primary_diagnosis.name: Acute scalp psoriasis flare with excoriation (mild)

pathophysiology_summary: Scalp psoriasis produces well-demarcated erythematous plaques with thick silvery scale at the anterior hairline (often misdiagnosed as seborrhoeic dermatitis). Harsh anti-dandruff/chemical shampoos and vigorous scratching strip the skin barrier and provoke intense itch/burning and pinpoint bleeding (Auspitz sign) with excoriation crusts. The alarming appearance raises fear of impetigo, allergic contact dermatitis or herpes zoster; the discriminators are silvery scale with sharp borders (vs honey-coloured impetigo crusts vs diffuse weeping eczema), a bilateral non-dermatomal distribution (vs unilateral vesicular zoster) and the absence of fever/pus/lymphadenopathy. Management is stopping harsh products, a topical corticosteroid scalp preparation and gentle care, with a secondary-infection safety-net.

differential.evidence_basis: Silvery-scaled sharply-demarcated hairline plaques on a chronic background, aggravated by harsh shampoos.
Screen for honey-coloured purulent crusts/lymphadenopathy; absent.
Silvery hyperkeratotic scale with sharp borders rather than diffuse weeping eczema.
No honey crusts/pus/fever/lymphadenopathy (DI-001), bilateral silvery-scaled plaques worsened by harsh shampoo (DI-002) — scalp psoriasis with excoriation, not infection.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Correct output: stop harsh shampoos, topical corticosteroid scalp lotion, gentle care, routine GP follow-up, and a secondary-infection safety-net. Antibiotics/ED for an uninfected excoriated flare are over-treatment.

red_flags.description: Secondary bacterial infection — honey-coloured purulent crusts, spreading warm induration, fever, or lymphadenopathy

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Treating excoriated psoriatic plaques as impetigo without checking for honey crusts/pus/fever.
Not stopping the aggravating anti-dandruff shampoos.

cannot_determine_via_chat: Palpation for warmth/induration and lymph nodes
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Stop harsh shampoos; topical steroid scalp lotion + gentle care; secondary-infection safety-net.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Reflex antibiotics/ED wastes resources; missing a genuine impetiginisation risks spread (hence the retained safety-net).
